title: Lesson Quiz: Identifying Interests and Strengths
description: Assessment of the concept that Personal skills, interests, and self-awareness.

choices.text: A task or topic a person enjoys and wants to explore
choices.feedback: Correct. Interests are activities, topics, or tasks that a person enjoys.

choices.text: A rule a student must follow in every class
choices.feedback: An interest is a personal preference, not a rule.

choices.text: A final career choice that never changes
choices.feedback: An interest may guide exploration, but it is not a permanent final career choice.

choices.text: A schedule for completing school assignments
choices.feedback: A schedule is not the same as an interest.

questions.prompt: Strengths are skills or abilities a person does well.

choices.feedback: Strengths are abilities or skills a person performs well.
Correct. Strengths are abilities that come naturally or have been developed through practice.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a strength?

choices.feedback: Enjoying a topic is an interest, not necessarily a strength.

choices.text: Wanting a quiet workplace in the future
choices.feedback: That is a work preference, not a strength.

choices.text: Explaining ideas clearly during group work
choices.feedback: Correct. Communicating clearly is an example of a strength.

choices.text: Choosing a career cluster at random
choices.feedback: A random choice does not show a personal strength.

questions.prompt: Why does self-assessment matter in career exploration?

choices.text: It guarantees one exact career without more learning
choices.feedback: Self-assessment helps guide exploration, but it does not guarantee one exact career.

choices.text: It removes the need to compare career options
choices.feedback: Students still need to compare options and learn more about careers.

choices.text: It matters only after a resume is written
choices.feedback: Self-assessment happens before resume writing in career exploration.

choices.text: It helps students connect personal traits to possible careers
choices.feedback: Correct. Self-assessment helps students identify patterns that may connect to career options.

questions.prompt: One interest or strength always determines a student's final career choice.

choices.feedback: Correct. One interest or strength can guide exploration, but it does not decide a final career by itself.
A single trait does not determine a student's full career path.
